document.diagnosis: Iteration 19 was primarily a candidate-selection result, not a candidate-scarcity result: five-base banks covered 8/12 cases while base plurality solved only 1/12, and nine-base banks covered 9/12 while nine-bank one-review repair solved 6/12. Five-bank three-review repair led at 7/12 with a 50% family floor and six helpful versus zero harmful interventions. Regenerate-then-repair remains promising in aggregate but showed severe integration variance: despite three sequence answers being present in its base bank, it selected none. The next checkpoint should preserve both champions, replicate regeneration unchanged, and use five-bank one-review repair as the clean reviewer-dilution and call-efficiency probe.
most_important_evidence: Five-bank three-review repair scored 7/12 in Iteration 19, with 6 helpful and 0 harmful interventions, versus a 1/12 base plurality; every family reached at least 2/4.
Nine-bank one-review repair scored 6/12 with 5 helpful and 0 harmful interventions, but converted only 6 of 9 oracle-covered cases and fell to 1/4 on logic.

Including Iteration 19, nine-bank one-review repair is 27/54 exact across its operationally equivalent runs; the current panel nevertheless shows that its family balance and candidate selection remain unsettled.

Regenerate-then-repair is now 21/48 exact, with expanded-oracle coverage of 25/48 versus base-oracle coverage of 19/48, and 17 helpful versus 2 harmful interventions. Its six-case coverage gain is real development evidence, but final selection remains variable.

In Iteration 19, regenerate-then-repair solved all 4 logic cases but 0/4 sequence cases even though the sequence base bank covered 3/4. This argues for unchanged replication rather than a family-specific modification.

Five-bank one-review repair previously reached 11/18 with 8 helpful and 0 harmful interventions in Iteration 15. It directly tests whether one reviewer retains most correction benefit while saving two calls per case relative to three-review repair.

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the minimum-call control unchanged so panel hardness and orchestration gains remain identifiable.
proposals.name: Direct One

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the deterministic five-answer plurality control unchanged to measure the value of candidate selection and answer-generating intervention.
proposals.name: Five-Vote Plurality

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the pooled champion unchanged. A broader independent bank paired with one falsifying repairer tests whether candidate breadth can be integrated without reviewer-panel dilution.
proposals.name: One-Review Nine-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the latest-panel winner unchanged. Its three parallel falsifying repairs provide the principal replicated answer-generating review control.
proposals.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Holding the five-answer bank and falsifying repair instructions fixed while reducing three reviewers to one tests whether extra reviewers dilute good corrections. It is also the clean call-efficiency probe against five-bank three-review repair.
proposals.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the candidate-generation pipeline unchanged on the larger sealed checkpoint. The test is whether its established expanded-oracle gain continues and whether one falsifying integrator reliably converts that gain across families.
proposals.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration

research_hypotheses: If reviewer dilution caused prior variability, five-bank one-review repair should preserve a positive correction balance while approaching or exceeding five-bank three-review accuracy with two fewer calls per case.
Comparing the two one-review systems isolates whether four additional base answers improve exact accuracy and family balance enough to justify their extra calls.
If blind regeneration is a transferable candidate-generation primitive, its expanded-oracle advantage over the shared five-answer bank should persist on 18 fresh cases.
If integration rather than coverage is the dominant bottleneck, oracle-to-final conversion and harmful reversals will distinguish the repair systems more strongly than raw bank size.
document.risk_note: This six-strategy batch costs at most 324 Luna calls under the shared-bank accounting rule: 18×9=162 base calls plus 18×(1+3+1+4)=162 follow-up calls, leaving 176 calls of headroom. Panel-to-panel and family-level variance remain large, so no single 18-case result should be treated as validation. Pool results strictly by operational fields, and separately track base oracle, expanded oracle, helpful corrections, harmful reversals, and family floors.
